steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The passage describes two features of black swan family structure: successful pairs foster cygnets of less successful pairs, and male-male pairs (not just female-male) rear cygnets — and do so with higher survival rates. The blank must name the property that these examples together illustrate: the capacity to take different forms and still function successfully.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Substitute each word and test it against the passage evidence:
  • "cohesion": Cohesion means unity or sticking together as a group — but the passage highlights varying structures, not unified solidarity. ✗
  • "elasticity": Elasticity means the ability to bend, adapt, and return to function — exactly what fostering across pairs and male-male rearing demonstrate. ✓
  • "affection": Affection means emotional fondness — the passage describes structural arrangements, not emotional bonds. ✗
  • "reciprocity": Reciprocity means mutual exchange — the text does not establish that fostering is given back in kind. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
